steps.title: Separate full sentences
steps.content: The first independent clause says Okinaka does not make decisions alone. The next independent clause says all historical designations require approval from nine other experts. A conjunctive adverb such as "however" cannot by itself join two complete clauses with only a comma.

steps.title: Avoid run-ons
steps.content: Choice B leaves the two clauses run together without proper punctuation. Choice C still joins two independent clauses with a comma and "however," creating a run-on. Choice D uses "and" with "however," producing an illogical and ungrammatical connection. The answer is A.
